title: The Archive's Freezer Rooms

features.name: the preservation racks
features.desc-on-touch: Files stored in rows: photographs in frost-sealed sleeves, recordings in condensation-jeweled cases. The archive keeps what it has learned to forget the warmth of.

features.name: the forgetting thermostat
features.desc-on-touch: A dial that reads temperatures in units the archive invented: degrees of lost warmth, calibrated to what hunger learns to remember as cold.

features.name: the condensation pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water that has run from the cold walls and gathered. It reflects nothing. When you touch it, you remember what it was before it became cold.

features.name: the sealed door
features.desc-on-touch: An archway into a chamber even colder than this one. The air flows from it carries the smell of preservation so perfect it stops being smell at all.

inscription: in the cold, the archive learns what it means to stop wanting

file.name: Preservation_Log_ChamberB_19882025.csv
file.note: Temperature records and contents list. Entries begin precise and gradually become poetic. Final row reads 'Temperature: the absence of the day you left.'

exits.label: back up into the kitchen's steam and hunger
down into the frost-core, where ice is not cold
